lesson.title: Graphs and Trees
lesson.explanation: A graph G=(V,E) consists of vertices and edges. Directed graphs have one-way edges; undirected have two-way. A tree is a connected acyclic graph. BFS (breadth-first search) explores level by level using a queue — finds shortest paths in unweighted graphs. DFS (depth-first search) explores as deep as possible using a stack — detects cycles, topological sort. Dijkstra's algorithm finds shortest paths in weighted graphs. Minimum spanning trees (Kruskal/Prim) connect all vertices with minimum total edge weight.
